summary: One house = smart. Two = rich. Three = fool.” and “Gold is insurance, not investment.”, Veteran investor Lakshmi Iyer (Group President, Bajaj Finserv & CEO, Bajaj Alternates; ex-Kotak) drops hard truths on real estate, gold, equity, debt, risk, discipline, and how to actually beat inflation. If you’ve ever asked “Where should I put my money?”, this episode is your blueprint.

seo.title: Where to Really Invest in 2025: Equity, Debt, Gold or Real Estate?
seo.description: One house = smart. Two = rich. Three = fool.” and “Gold is insurance, not investment.”, Veteran investor Lakshmi Iyer (Group President, Bajaj Finserv &

takeaways: Gold is framed as insurance, not investment: it protects your portfolio against shocks but rarely compounds wealth, so it plays defense rather than driving your growth.

The episode reframes real estate with a blunt line, one house is smart, two is rich, three is a fool, and separates when it actually makes sense to buy a home from when to get property exposure through markets like REITs.
Long term is not a number of years, it is defined by market cycles and your tolerance for shocks, which changes how you judge whether you are being patient or just stuck.
The biggest investor mistakes here are behavioral, not technical: fear, greed, and killing your SIPs at the wrong moment, while rebalancing and discipline are the boring edge that quietly wins.
Asset allocation is presented as a simple framework where equity, debt and gold each do a different job, paired with a blended-return mental model for thinking about how to stay ahead of inflation.
